title: Decimal to binary

content.problemStatement: Write a program to input an integer N in decimal form, convert it into binary, and then print the result.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (in decimal).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the binary representation of N without leading zeros on a separate line.

solution.approach: The binary representation of a non‑negative integer can be obtained by repeatedly dividing the number by 2 and collecting the remainders. The remainders read in reverse order form the binary string. For N=0 the answer is "0". This process takes O(log₂N) steps because the number of divisions equals the number of bits. Since each test case is independent we apply the same procedure for all T cases.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T·log N) – each test case needs at most log₂N divisions (or a single library call).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the output string.
